genderRules.title: People commonly marked masculine
genderRules.description: Many nouns for male people are masculine.

genderRules.title: People commonly marked feminine
genderRules.description: Many nouns for female people are feminine.

genderRules.title: Nouns ending in -ung are feminine
genderRules.description: Nouns ending in -ung are usually feminine.

genderRules.title: Nouns ending in -ei are feminine
genderRules.description: Nouns ending in -ei are usually feminine.

genderRules.title: Some international / neutral borrowed nouns are neuter
genderRules.description: Some common borrowed nouns like Hotel or Büro are neuter.

genderRules.title: Exception
genderRules.description: This noun should be treated as an exception for study purposes.

pluralRules.title: Plural with -e
pluralRules.description: Many nouns form the plural with -e.

pluralRules.title: Plural with -er and often umlaut
pluralRules.description: Some common nouns take -er and often add an umlaut.

pluralRules.title: Plural with -en / -n
pluralRules.description: Many feminine nouns and some others take -en or -n.

pluralRules.title: Plural with -s
pluralRules.description: Some borrowed nouns and abbreviations take -s.

pluralRules.title: Plural with no ending change
pluralRules.description: Some nouns keep the same form in singular and plural.

pluralRules.title: Exception
pluralRules.description: This plural should be treated as an exception for study purposes.
